work_context: A small operations team receives a polished AI-assisted project brief before deciding whether it is safe to forward to a partner.

artifact.title: Q3 community workshop expansion brief
artifact.source: AI-assisted draft from team notes and public website copy

intended_use: Internal planning only; not partner-facing until unsupported claims and ownership questions are resolved.

review_checks.evidence: Attendance estimate appears in the draft but no source note or prior registration report is attached.
Draft says 'the team will follow up' without naming the person who owns partner contact.
No client names, private records, financial account data, or health/legal details are included.
Two confident recommendations rely on unverified demand assumptions.

known_weak_spots: No note about what changed from the original notes
human_edits: Changed 'will increase turnout' to 'may increase turnout if partner demand is confirmed'
Added owner placeholder and source-needed marker
do_not_claim: This receipt does not prove the brief is accurate
This is not an AI detector or worker discipline tool
Do not treat polished formatting as review evidence
boundary_note: Local review hygiene aid only. It is not legal, HR, compliance, quality-certification, or performance-management advice. Use it to reduce downstream burden, not to blame the person who drafted the work.
